item.title: World Bank delegation calls on social welfare minister
item.description: A three-member World Bank delegation led by divisional director for Bangladesh and Bhutan Jean Pesme met with the social welfare and women and children affairs minister Professor AZM Zahid Hossain and state minister for women......Political commentary Bangladesh

item.title: ICT-2 begins trial of former DMP chief Habibur, 10 other officers
item.description: The International Crimes Tribunal-2 on Sunday began the trial of former Dhaka Metropolitan Police commissioner Habibur Rahman and 10 other police officers over the killing of protesters, including college student Imam Hasan Taim, in the capital’s Jatrabari......

item.title: Agri labour wages rise slightly, women see lower growth
item.description: The agricultural labour wages increased slightly in the final quarter (October-December) of 2025 compared with those in the previous quarter....

item.title: BMU discusses AI-based cardiac solution
item.description: As part of the first International Heart Failure Conference 2026 to be held in Dhaka, a pre-conference workshop was held at the capital’s BMU Super Specialised Hospital on Sunday....

item.title: Israeli strike on ambulance in Lebanon kills paramedic
item.description: An Israeli strike on an ambulance belonging to the Hezbollah-affiliated Islamic Health Committee killed one paramedic and his patient Sunday, the group’s spokesperson said....City & Local Guides

item.title: 153 gold bars recovered from Biman aircraft at HSIA
item.description: Members of intelligence agencies and the Aviation Security in a joint drive on Sunday recovered 153 gold bars, equivalent to about 17.7 kilograms of gold, from a Biman Bangladesh Airlines aircraft at the Hazrat Shahjalal International Airport in the capital Dhaka....

item.title: Iran responds to Trump’s 15-point plan via Islamabad
item.description: Iran on Sunday conveyed its response to a 15-point US proposal to end the ongoing Middle East war through Islamabad, as Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Egypt and Turkey held talks about trying to end the Gulf war....

item.title: RMG may face headwinds in next quarters: BB
item.description: The export performance of the country’s readymade garment sector might face growing headwinds in the coming quarters, said a report published by the Bangladesh Bank on Sunday......

item.title: Bangladesh loses $68.3b in 10 years in hidden trade flows: GFI report
item.description: Bangladesh lost an estimated $68.3 billion, or about Tk 8.37 lakh crore, through trade-related illicit financial flows between 2013 and 2022, according to a report by Global Financial Integrity released on 26 March......

item.title: Measles death toll rises to 44 as infections surge
item.description: Health division officials on Sunday said that at least 44 children had died of measles across Bangladesh this year as new fatalities and previously unreported cases continued to emerge....

item.title: Children’s hospital in Rajshahi lies idle 3 years after construction
item.description: A 200-bed government children’s hospital in Rajshahi has remained non-operational nearly three years after completion of construction, even as critically ill children continue to die at Rajshahi Medical College Hospital amid a severe shortage of intensive care facilities....

item.title: Senegal on edge of collapse
item.description: Senegal entered 2026 in the grip of a growing debt crisis that seems insurmountable. After the government of president Bassirou Diomaye Faye took power in April 2024, it became clear that his predecessor, Macky Sall (who held office from 2012 to 2024), had concealed enormous liabilities — including hidden loans equivalent to 25.3 percent of GDP — from the......

item.title: Apology for lottery-based school admission
item.description: UP UNTIL 2010, children were admitted to schools through gruelling admission tests which forced the children into an academic marathon long before they understood the immediate rewards and long-term repercussions. This admission system, later, fell into disfavour as educationists identified major limitations in it and expressed concerns. Consequently, the......

item.title: Where did sparrows go?
item.description: MANY long-time residents of Dhaka remember living with permanent house guests who were not relatives but sparrows. In houses with ventilators, wooden beams, or tiled roofs, these birds nested above door frames or inside wall crevices. They fluttered in and out freely, sometimes venturing into rooms and prompting a sudden rush to switch off ceiling fans to keep......Geographic Reference

item.title: Rethinking Dhaka’s approach
item.description: SINCE the beginning of the war on Iran on February 28, 2026, at least seven Bangladeshis have been killed in West Asian states: three in Saudi Arabia and one each in Iraq, the United Arab Emirates, Kuwait and Bahrain. Meanwhile, in spite of the war, Bangladeshi expatriates sent $2.2 billion in remittances in the first two weeks of March, up 36 per cent from......

item.title: Executive excuses to control judiciary unacceptable
item.description: THE passage of the Supreme Court Secretariat Ordinance 2025 into law has run into problems as the law, justice and parliamentary affairs ministry has voiced objections. The legislation, promulgated on November 30, 2025, is meant to grant administrative and judicial independence to the judiciary. The independence of the judiciary was officially effected......

item.title: Unattended gap leading to measles outbreak deplorable
item.description: AN OUTBREAK of measles, a highly contagious viral infection, across the country is an indication of a weakening immunisation programme. At least 35 children have died from measles and several hundred others have been infected across the country since January. On March 28, about 30 patients were undergoing treatment in Bangladesh Shishu Hospital, with one new......Bangladesh news updates

item.title: Spl committee completes review of 133 ordinances amid ruling-opposition rift
item.description: A parliamentary special committee on Sunday completed its review of 133 ordinances issued during the interim government, amid disagreements between the ruling party and the opposition over several key provisions. ...

item.title: Russian tanker nears Cuba, defying US oil blockade
item.description: A Russian oil tanker subject to US sanctions is scheduled to arrive in Cuba on Monday, challenging a de facto American fuel blockade of the energy-starved island, shipping data shows....

item.title: Card to be mandatory to buy fuel in Chuadanga
item.description: THE district administration in Chuadanga on Sunday decided to introduce a fuel card system, making it mandatory for vehicles to present a card to purchase fuel in a bid to ensure normal supply and prevent irregularities....

item.title: Nepal court extends former PM detention
item.description: A Nepal court on Sunday extended by five days the detention of former prime minister KP Sharma Oli and his home minister after their arrest over alleged involvement in a deadly crackdown on the 2025 protests that ousted him....

item.title: One killed in landslide during extracting sand from riverbank
item.description: A day labourer was killed in a landslide early Sunday morning while he was extracting sand cutting riverbank at Tahirpur upazila in Sunamganj....

item.title: 9 rly employees, bus driver found responsible
item.description: The report of the investigation committee formed by the district administration, over a train-bus collision in Cumilla that left 12 people dead, found nine railway employees and the bus driver responsible for the incident....

item.title: Ukraine strike sparks fire at major Russian Baltic port
item.description: A drone strike claimed by Ukraine triggered a fire at Russia’s Baltic port of Ust-Luga as the regional governor on Sunday reported new damage at the major exporting hub hit for a second time in days....

item.title: Ministers pledge priority for rural infrastructure, public services
item.description: Local government, rural development and cooperatives minister Mirza Fakhrul Islam Alamgir has assured that rural roads and communication networks will receive the highest priority in the coming days, with zero tolerance for corruption....

item.title: Parties urge upholding ‘71 spirit, resisting anti-liberation forces
item.description: Political leaders from ruling and opposition parties on Sunday, at a discussion meeting in Dhaka, urged that the Bangladesh Nationalist Party government must work to establish the spirit of the 1971 liberation war and that both sides should work......Maps

item.title: Man stabbed by brother dies in Naogaon
item.description: A man who was critically injured during a family dispute at Manda in Naogaon has died while undergoing treatment in Rajshahi Medical College Hospital on Saturday evening....

item.title: Body of missing brick kiln worker recovered in Noakhali
item.description: The police recovered body of a brick kiln worker from a water body in Noakhali’s Kabirhat upazila on Sunday morning, two days after he went missing....

item.title: Nasiruddin appointed DMP spokesperson
item.description: Deputy commissioner of police NM Nasiruddin has been appointed as the spokesperson of the Dhaka Metropolitan Police....

item.title: Field officials must meet public expectations in land services: minister
item.description: Land minister Md Mizanur Rahman Minu on Sunday urged the field-level officials across the country to align their work with public expectations while delivering land-related services....Bangladesh news updates

item.title: Home minister seeks French support for police reform, capacity building
item.description: Home minister Salahuddin Ahmed has sought France’s cooperation in police reform and capacity building....

item.title: Severed head recovered in Manikganj
item.description: The police on Sunday recovered the severed head of a youth, assumed to be battery-run rickshaw driver Rafiq Mia, from a corn field in Manikganj....

item.title: Use of environment friendly building materials stressed at workshop
item.description: Speakers at a workshop in Dhaka on Sunday stressed the need for circular building materials designed for longevity, reuse and recycling, aiming to reduce carbon emissions, promote sustainable growth and ensure resource-efficient practices......

item.title: Woman, baby girl among five die in road accidents
item.description: At least five people were killed and 10 more were injured in road crashes in four districts, namely, Barishal, Dinajpur, Meherpur and Bogura, on Sunday....

item.title: SSC, EQUIVALENT EXAMS: CCTV cameras to be installed at all rooms
item.description: Closed-circuit television cameras are going to be installed in every room of the examination centres where the coming Secondary School Certificate and equivalent examinations will be held....

item.title: Turning ordinances into laws demanded
item.description: Rights activists and family members of victims of enforced disappearance have urged the government to immediately turn the Enforced Disappearance Prevention and Remedies Ordinance, 2025 and the National Human Rights Commission Ordinance, 2025......

item.title: Five Eid films competing well
item.description: Films released on Saturday during the Eid-ul-Fitr holidays mostly received an overwhelming response from audiences....

item.title: Establishing society free of discrimination real goal of independence: DU VC
item.description: Newly-appointed vice-chancellor of the University of Dhaka Professor ABM Obaidul Islam on Sunday said that establishing a discrimination-free society was the true objective of independence....Maps

item.title: WB delegation calls on agriculture minister
item.description: A World Bank delegation led by Bangladesh and Bhutan division director Jean Pesme paid a courtesy call on agriculture and fisheries and livestock minister Mohammad Aminur Rashid at the conference room of the agriculture ministry in Dhaka on Sunday. ...
